2606.12599.title: Constrained Semantic Decompression in LLMs through Persian Proverb-Conditioned Story Generation
2606.12599.paper_summary: Transforming a dense, abstract proverb into an engaging and morally faithful narrative requires deep cultural understanding and robust semantic grounding. We frame this problem as a \emph{constrained semantic decompression} task and study proverb-conditioned story generation as a testbed for abstraction-to-realization in large language models (LLMs). Focusing on Persian, we introduce the Proverb Aligned Narrative Dataset (PAND), pairing proverbs with human-written stories and explicit meanings. By a hybrid evaluation framework that combines human-calibrated LLM-as-a-Judge with structural metrics, we analyze model behavior across multiple prompting regimes. Our findings reveal a persistent \emph{decompression gap}: current LLMs often achieve strong surface-level fluency while failing to faithfully instantiate the underlying moral and causal structure encoded in proverbs. We further show that explicit reasoning and iterative refinement can partially mitigate these failures, suggesting that many decompression errors arise from difficulties in translating abstract meaning into narrative form rather than a complete lack of relevant knowledge. Our proposed task naturally extends to other forms of compressed cultural knowledge.
2606.12599.paper_summary_zh: 將一個密集、抽象的諺語轉化為引人入勝且道德上忠實的敘事需要深厚的文化理解和堅實的語義基礎。<lb/>我們將這個問題框架設為一個\emph{受限語義解壓}任務，並將基於諺語的故事生成作為大型語言模型（LLMs）中抽象到實現的測試平台。<lb/>專注於波斯語，我們介紹了諺語對齊敘事數據集（PAND），將諺語與人類撰寫的故事和明確的意義配對。<lb/>通過一個混合評估框架，結合了人類校準的LLM作為評判者和結構性指標，我們分析了模型在多種提示模式下的行為。<lb/>我們的發現揭示了一個持久的\emph{解壓差距}：當前的LLMs通常在表面流暢性上表現強勁，但未能忠實地體現諺語中編碼的道德和因果結構。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，明確的推理和迭代的精煉可以部分減輕這些失敗，這表明許多解壓錯誤源於將抽象意義轉化為敘事形式的困難，而不是完全缺乏相關知識。<lb/>我們提出的任務自然擴展到其他形式的壓縮文化知識。
2606.12599.author: Zahra Habibzadeh et.al.
2606.12599.authors: Zahra Habibzadeh, Paria Khoshtab, Amir Mesbah, Yadollah Yaghoobzadeh
